title: Serving Low-Latency Data with Hybrid Tables
author: Adam Timm
description: Replace reverse ETL pipelines with Hybrid Tables as a low-latency serving layer for email personalization, API backends, and application workloads.

scenarios.name: Email/Marketing Personalization
scenarios.pattern: Precompute recommendations in standard table, CTAS+SWAP into Hybrid Table, serve at high concurrency to email platform
scenarios.access_pattern: Lookup by user_id (PK prefix), return top N recommendations

scenarios.name: API Backend (Entitlements/Session State)
scenarios.pattern: Hybrid Table as REST API backing store for authorization decisions
scenarios.access_pattern: Lookup by composite key (user_id, resource_type, resource_id)

steps: CREATE OR REPLACE HYBRID TABLE new_table (...) AS SELECT * FROM source
ALTER TABLE serving_table SWAP WITH new_table
ctas_swap_pattern.why_swap: ALTER TABLE SWAP WITH is atomic metadata operation. Applications see new data immediately with zero downtime. RENAME requires two statements with a brief name-unavailable window.
ctas_swap_pattern.compaction_note: After CTAS+SWAP, the new table requires a warm-up period (plan cache, warehouse data cache). Schedule refreshes during low-traffic windows to minimize impact.

warehouse_sizing.size: XSMALL (point lookups don't need compute power)
warehouse_sizing.why_xs: XS cluster is sufficient for many operational serving workloads. Benchmark your patterns, then scale horizontally via clusters, not vertically via larger size.

warpspeed.note: WarpSpeed (Public Preview) can improve latency for repeated query patterns. Benchmark your specific workload to measure impact.
warpspeed.serving_fit: High — serving workloads execute the same parameterized query shape repeatedly

validated_patterns: ALTER TABLE SWAP WITH on Hybrid Tables
Point lookup on composite PK prefix
